howItWorks.title: How the AI Music Video Generator from Lyrics Works

steps.title: Upload your song
steps.description: Drop an MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, or M4A file (up to 100MB). No account needed to start, and no manual lyric typing required. Your song's lyrics become the creative source for everything that follows.

steps.title: AI transcribes and generates visuals
steps.description: AI transcribes lyrics from the audio automatically and syncs every line to the beat — in 50+ languages. Then it reads the song's mood, themes, and emotional arc to generate matching visual scenes. Click any lyric line to edit.

steps.title: Preview and export
steps.description: Watch the full AI music video before publishing. Confirm the visuals match your song's energy, then export in 16:9 for YouTube, 9:16 for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts, or 1:1 for feed posts. Free to export, no subscription required.

whyChoose.title: Why Make AI Music Videos on LyricVideoMaker
whyChoose.description: Generic AI video tools make you describe scenes in a text box. LyricVideoMaker starts from your actual song — the AI reads the lyrics and generates visuals that match the mood, so the music video feels like it belongs to the track.

highlight.title: Visuals generated from your lyrics, not a text prompt
highlight.description: The AI uses the meaning, mood, and emotional arc of your lyrics to drive scene generation. You don't write a video prompt — you upload a song.

cards.title: Scene ideas come from the song
cards.description: The visual direction is based on lyrical meaning, mood, and emotional movement instead of a separate prompt you have to invent.

cards.title: Lyrics drive the visuals
cards.description: The AI reads what your song is about and generates scenes that match — not random stock footage, not generic motion graphics. Visuals that feel like your song.

cards.title: Words can stay visible
cards.description: You can keep lyrics on screen while AI scenes play behind them, which helps the result stay useful for fans who want to follow the song.

contentSections.heading: What is an AI music video generator from lyrics?
contentSections.intro: An AI music video generator from lyrics uses the song to plan visual scenes instead of treating lyrics only as on-screen text. LyricVideoMaker starts from audio, transcribes and syncs the lyrics, reads the mood and story, generates scene images, and lets you preview how visual storytelling and lyric readability work together.
bullets: Input is a song audio file, with lyrics transcribed automatically from the vocal.
It can build scene-by-scene visual ideas from mood, imagery, structure, and energy.
Output is an MP4, with aspect ratios for YouTube and short-form platforms.
It differs from a standard lyric video because scenes can become the visual lead, while lyrics may remain readable or secondary.

contentSections.heading: AI music video generator from lyrics specs at a glance

rows: Accepted input
Audio upload only: MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, or M4A, up to 100MB per file.
Lyrics
AI transcribes vocals automatically in 50+ languages, then lets you edit every line before export.
Sync
Beat-aware line timing keeps lyrics readable and musical instead of simply dropping captions on a fixed grid.
Visuals
Storyboard planning and AI scene-image generation create backgrounds around the mood of the song.
Exports
MP4 with original audio in 16:9, 9:16, or 1:1 for YouTube, Shorts, TikTok, Reels, and feeds.
Start
Free credits are available at the start, so you can preview the workflow before spending more time on a release.

contentSections.heading: Lyric video vs AI music video
contentSections.intro: The most important decision is whether the words or the scenes should be the main visual. Both can use AI, but they solve different release needs.

rows: Lyric video
Words are the primary visual; scenes support readability and rhythm.
AI music video
Scenes are the primary visual; lyrics may guide the story or appear selectively.
Best for lyric channels
Lyric video, because fans came to read and sing along.
Best for storytelling
AI music video, because imagery can carry characters, symbols, and mood.
Main risk
Lyric videos fail when text is hard to read; music videos fail when scenes feel random.
Best hybrid
Use lyric-video clarity for hooks and AI music-video scenes for instrumental or narrative sections.

contentSections.heading: Best practices for AI music videos from lyrics
contentSections.intro: Use these checks before you render, especially when the same project needs more than one platform format.
bullets: Decide whether the lyric or the scene is the hero before generating visuals.
Use repeated symbols from the lyrics to keep scenes connected.
Choose atmosphere-driven styles when exact character continuity is not essential.
Review every generated scene before final render; one wrong scene can break the story.
Keep lyrics readable during hooks if fans are expected to sing along.
Use 16:9 for official visualizers and 9:16 for story-focused short clips.
Treat the first preview as a storyboard, then regenerate only the scenes that miss the song.

contentSections.heading: Where AI visuals help, and where lyrics need discipline
contentSections.intro: An AI music video generator turns a song into scene-driven visuals while keeping lyrics readable. The choices that matter happen before you render — here are the principles worth keeping.

cards.title: Mood travels better than exact characters
cards.description: Scene generation can build a strong world around a song, but keeping a character identical across every shot is harder than holding mood, palette, and symbols steady — so review scenes in preview.

cards.title: Lyrics are structure, not decoration
cards.description: The words are the structure the viewer follows, not a caption added last. Always transcribe first, align to the beat second, and design the scene third.

cards.title: AI does the heavy lifting, you keep the taste
cards.description: AI handles transcription, timing, and scene generation. Whether the style fits, a line break should change, or you need a vertical, square, or widescreen export stays your call.

cards.title: The scene serves the lyric
cards.description: Viewers should never choose between reading the line and watching the scene. Keep contrast strong, lines short, and motion restrained so the visuals support the words.

cards.title: Review in three passes
cards.description: Watch once for words to catch transcription errors, once for rhythm to catch lines that arrive early or late, and once for platform fit so it still reads in a phone feed.

cards.title: One source, every format
cards.description: A single project becomes a 16:9 full version, a 9:16 vertical, and a 1:1 square without retyping lyrics or rebuilding timing — which is what makes a release feel planned.

cards.title: Build it while the song is fresh
cards.description: Make the lyric video while the song is still in your ear: you will hear awkward line breaks faster, catch misheard words sooner, and judge where the energy should rise or stay quiet.

contentSections.heading: AI music video generator from lyrics checklist before export
contentSections.intro: A quick pre-export review catches most quality problems while they are still cheap to fix.

rows: Audio readiness
Accepted formats are MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, and M4A; keep the file under 100MB.
Language review
The system auto-detects 50+ languages, but bilingual hooks and names deserve a manual pass.
Timing review
Beat sync gives a strong baseline; nudge any line that lands early or late against the vocal.
Scene review
Generated images should match the mood without making text hard to read.
Export review
Check 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1 separately because readable text size changes by format.

items.question: Is this AI music video generator free?
items.answer: Yes. Sign up, get free credits, and export your AI music video — no subscription required, no credit card needed.

items.question: How does AI generate music video visuals from lyrics?
items.answer: The AI reads the mood, themes, and emotional arc of your lyrics and generates matching visual scenes. Each scene reflects what the song is about, not a generic prompt.

items.question: Do I need to type the lyrics myself?
items.answer: No. Upload your song and AI transcribes every lyric line for you in 50+ languages — the same lyrics then drive the generated scenes. Edit any line if needed.

items.question: What is the difference between a lyric video and an AI music video?
items.answer: In a lyric video, the words are the main visual. In an AI music video, the lyrics inspire scene-by-scene imagery and visuals. LyricVideoMaker can do both — the lyrics stay on screen while AI-generated visuals play behind them.

items.question: Can I use exported music videos commercially?
items.answer: Yes. The AI music videos you export are yours, monetized channels and paid releases included. Just make sure you own the rights to the song behind them.

items.question: What formats can I export in?
items.answer: Export 16:9 for YouTube, 9:16 for TikTok, Reels, and Shorts, or 1:1 for feed posts — every format from one song upload.

items.question: Will the characters look the same in every scene?
items.answer: Expect a consistent style and mood across scenes rather than identical characters frame to frame. The AI matches your song's look and feel — and you can regenerate any scene you want to adjust.
